title: rhynchosia tomentosa var. mollissima
intro: Rhynchosia tomentosa var. mollissima, also known as the hairy bean or velvet bush bean, is a plant species native to tropical Africa and Madagascar, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Rhynchosia tomentosa var. mollissima include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often dried and used as a tea or made into a paste for topical application.
